---
title: Counterfactual Explanations and Algorithmic Recourse for Trustworthy AI
subtitle: Research Seminar
author: 
  - name: "**Patrick Altmeyer**"
institute: Delft University of Technology
date: 2026-03-11
format: 
  tudelft-revealjs:
    theme: dark
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    html-math-method: mathjax
    auto-animate-easing: ease-in-out
    auto-animate-unmatched: false
    auto-animate-duration: 0.8
  beamer: 
    slide-level: 2
    theme: Berlin
    colortheme: albatross
    fontsize: 10pt
---

## The Ground Truth (Reality) {auto-animate=true}

![Predictors of default risk.](www/presentation/s1.png){#fig-pred width="60%"}

## The Ground Truth (Reality) {auto-animate=true} 

![Ground truth outcomes across two predictors.](www/presentation/s2.png){#fig-gt width="60%"}

## Black-Box AI {auto-animate=true}

![Classifier predicts correctly 8 out of 10 times.](www/presentation/s3.png){#fig-clf width="60%"}

## Black-Box AI {auto-animate=true}

![Simple counterfactual explanation for the black-box AI. [JuliaCon Proceedings 2023](https://proceedings.juliacon.org/papers/10.21105/jcon.00130.pdf)](www/presentation/s4.png){#fig-ce width="60%"}

## Black-Box AI {auto-animate=true}

![One happy recourse recipient, many losers. [IEEE SaTML 2023](https://www.computer.org/csdl/proceedings-article/satml/2023/629900a418/1NCHGSQ0uNG)](www/presentation/s5.png){#fig-ar width="60%"}

## Black-Box AI {auto-animate=true}

![Plausible counterfactual explanations for the black-box AI.  [IEEE SaTML 2023](https://www.computer.org/csdl/proceedings-article/satml/2023/629900a418/1NCHGSQ0uNG)](www/presentation/s6.png){#fig-plausible-ce width="60%"}

## Black-Box AI {auto-animate=true}

![One somewhat happy recourse recipient, no losers.  [IEEE SaTML 2023](https://www.computer.org/csdl/proceedings-article/satml/2023/629900a418/1NCHGSQ0uNG)](www/presentation/s7.png){#fig-plausible-ar width="60%"}

## Big, Beautiful Black-Box AI {auto-animate=true}

![Classifier predicts correctly 9 out of 10 times. But ... [AAAI 2024](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/28956)](www/presentation/s8.png){#fig-big-clf width="60%"}

## Big, Beautiful Black-Box AI {auto-animate=true}

![Plausible counterfactual explanations remains valid. Happy days? [AAAI 2024](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/28956)](www/presentation/s9.png){#fig-big-plausible-ce width="60%"}

## Big, Beautiful Black-Box AI {auto-animate=true}

![White-washed black-box: plausible CE hides bias. [AAAI 2024](https://ojs.aaai.org/index.php/AAAI/article/view/28956)](www/presentation/s10.png){#fig-worst-case width="60%"}

## Holding Models Accountable {auto-animate=true}

![A model trained to use plausible explanations for predictions. [IEEE SaTML 2026](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=e7KRRa8AAAAJ&citation_for_view=e7KRRa8AAAAJ:4TOpqqG69KYC)](www/presentation/s11.png){#fig-best-case width="60%"}

## 'ok but agi bruh'

![My personal take on "AGI by 2027". [ICML 2024](https://dl.acm.org/doi/abs/10.5555/3692070.3692121)](www/presentation/s12.jpg){#fig-agi}

## In all seriousness ... {auto-animate=true}


:::{.incremental}
- Useful? Absolutely
- AGI? Sentient? Conscious? No: 'emergence' in complex systems does not hint at any of this
:::

## In all seriousness ... {auto-animate=true}


:::{.incremental}
- Emergence broadly described as broad behaviour of complex systems that's different from its constituent parts:
  - Example 1: asset price bubbles in financial markets -> locally predictable, rational behaviour, but also market failure
  - Example 2: tornado -> just dust and debris, but also a possible disaster
- Does it matter?
:::
